_note: Whitespace is allowed between the mustache '{' and the sigil that classifies the tag: the Svelte compiler's tag lexer advances past '{' and skips whitespace BEFORE dispatching on '#'/':'/'/'/'@', so '{ #if}', '{ :else}', '{ /if}', '{ @html}' and '{ const}' are all valid. The template parser dispatched on literal two-byte prefixes ('{:', '{/', '{#', '{@'), so '{ /if}' fell through to expression parsing where the '/' read as a regex opener, producing an unterminated-mustache error and dropping the rest of the fragment. The section pre-pass classifier already skipped the whitespace, so the two layers disagreed about the same bytes. All layers now share one sigil classifier.
